sourceMovie.title: Clear and Present Danger
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Clear and Present Danger (1994) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Phillip Noyce's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Bourne Ultimatum
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Clear and Present Danger clicked for you, The Bourne Ultimatum (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Clear and Present Danger?
faq.answer: Try The Bourne Ultimatum, Shooter, The Sum of All Fears—each shares DNA with Clear and Present Danger while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Clear and Present Danger?
faq.answer: Clear and Present Danger (1994) is best described as Action, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Clear and Present Danger part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Jack Ryan Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
